title: 3 things the Bruins must fix if they want to punch their ticket to the playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins' power play struggles, dropping to 28th in the league post-Olympic break, hinder playoff push.
Lindholm and Geekie contribute no points in 11 games, impacting Bruins' offensive depth.
Defensive inconsistencies with Peeke and Jokiharju rotation create issues for Bruins' third pairing.

title: 5 Red Sox takeaways as Wilyer Abreu and Eduardo Rodriguez lift Venezuela to World Baseball Classic title
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Wilyer Abreu hit a home run to help Venezuela win its first World Baseball Classic title over Team USA.
Former Red Sox pitcher Eduardo Rodriguez threw four and one-third scoreless innings in the championship game.
Red Sox reliever Garrett Whitlock allowed the winning run in the ninth inning, resulting in a 3-2 loss for Team USA.

title: This Ashland mom is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children with complex medical needs and their parents
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mengyuan Flanagan from Ashland, Massachusetts, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon supporting the Michael Lisnow Respite Center.
The Michael Lisnow Respite Center aids children with complex medical needs and provides relief for their families.
Flanagan began running three years ago, finding physical and mental health benefits, and is now raising funds for the center.

title: Western Mass. couple found guilty of manslaughter in death of foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adams couple found guilty of involuntary manslaughter in the death of their 10-month-old foster child.
Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker convicted after ignoring signs child needed medical treatment.
Sentencing scheduled for March 19; defendants face up to 20 years for manslaughter, 2.5 years for endangerment.

title: Major water main break in Jackson Square floods streets, causes detours
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A major water main break in Jamaica Plain flooded streets at Columbus Avenue and Centre and Ritchie streets.
Traffic was rerouted as Boston police managed congestion and the MBTA closed the Jackson Square busway temporarily.
Crews worked to shut off water affecting a vast area with three daycare businesses evacuated due to the flooding.

title: Why Jaylen Waddle’s trade to Broncos is bad news for the Patriots
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Waddle traded to Broncos from Dolphins for three 2026 picks, including a first-rounder.
Patriots face challenge as Broncos strengthen with Waddle, impacting New England's pursuit of A.J. Brown.
Philadelphia may demand a first-round pick for A.J. Brown after Broncos' deal for Waddle sets a precedent.

title: 2 charged after car hits pedestrians outside of downtown Boston nightclub
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Darius Boyce, 45, and Maria Moraes, 25, charged after Jeep hit three pedestrians outside ICON nightclub in Boston.
Boyce faces assault charges and improper firearm storage; Moraes charged with police interference and disorderly conduct.
Victims reported incident to police, leading to arrest after weapon and vehicle license information confirmed suspects.

title: Former President Biden makes surprise St. Patrick’s Day appearance in Boston
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former President Joe Biden made an unscheduled appearance at the Irish American Partnership’s St. Patrick’s Day Breakfast in Boston.
Biden's speech emphasized Irish American history, immigrant experiences, and criticized the Trump administration's immigration policies.
Governor Maura Healey joined Biden, highlighting Boston's celebration of Irish heritage and urging reflection on immigrant experiences.

title: 2 Boston College students injured in deck collapse during off-campus party
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Boston College students were injured after falling from a second-floor deck in Brighton during a party.
The deck railing broke, causing the students to fall onto concrete; rotting wood and corroded nails were observed.
Victims were treated at local hospitals; authorities continue investigating the incident at 64 Undine Road.

title: Patriots rookie earns hefty performance bonus after strong 2025 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots safety Craig Woodson started 15 games and played 950 snaps in 2025, leading the team's defense.
Woodson received a $1,149,910 performance-based bonus in 2025, added to his $840,000 base salary.
In 2025 playoffs, Woodson had 30 tackles, four tackles for loss, and an interception in four games.
